citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
One female mini poodle (microchip #94169, Tag 13) born on 07/19/2025 (9 months old) was identified by the inspector as
being severely underweight during the inspection on 7 April 2026. Since that inspection, the licensee stated he dewormed
the dog but could not get into the attending veterinarian to have the dog evaluated before the correction date given of
5:00pm of April 8. The dog's neck is extremely thin, the spinal vertebrae are clearly observed, and the pelvic bones are
protruding. The dog was physically examined by an Animal Care Inspector and two Veterinary Medical Officers. When the
inspectors physically examined the dog, her spine, hips, and ribs were easily seen and felt under the haircoat. The animal
was trembling and hunched over while being examined. At the time of this inspection, the dog still has not been evaluated
by a licensed veterinarian.
Failure to adequately observe animals daily to identify veterinary care conditions or communicating veterinary issues to a
veterinarian can lead to worsening of the condition, delay in treatments, or death. Correct by ensuring that the animals
and conditions listed above are reported to the attending veterinarian so that the animal can be properly examined,
diagnosed, and treated. Additionally, ensure that daily observation of animals is adequate to identify those signs of
abnormal health and behavior and that there is direct and frequent communication of those observations to the attending
veterinarian. Correct by 5:00 PM on 09-April-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
Additional Inspectors:
KATHRYN CAMPITELLI, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER
Jonathan Tomkovitch, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct Repeat
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
One female mini poodle (microchip #94169, Tag 13) born on 07/19/2025 (9 months old) was identified by the inspector as

inspectors physically examined the dog, her spine, hips, and ribs were easily seen and felt under the haircoat. The animal 
was trembling and hunched over while being examined. At the time of this inspection, the dog still has not been evaluated 
by a licensed veterinarian. 
 
Failure to adequately observe animals daily to identify veterinary care conditions or communicating veterinary issues to a
